seedPool.name: Snow
seedPool.description: The country's winter ground.
seedPool.narrative: It buries roads, silences forests, and lasts six months of the year.

seedPool.name: Ice
seedPool.description: Frozen lake, thick enough to walk on.
seedPool.narrative: Every winter the lakes harden into a second floor of the country.

seedPool.name: Birch
seedPool.description: Pale-barked tree of the northern forest.
seedPool.narrative: The first leaf in spring, the last log in the sauna stove.

seedPool.name: Pine
seedPool.description: Tall evergreen of the boreal woods.
seedPool.narrative: The tree that owns the country's silhouette.

seedPool.name: Lake
seedPool.description: One of nearly two hundred thousand.
seedPool.narrative: If the country were folded along its shores, every Finn would have a beach.

seedPool.name: Berry
seedPool.description: Wild fruit of the forest floor.
seedPool.narrative: Lingonberry, bilberry, cloudberry — picked by anyone, eaten by everyone.

seedPool.name: Fish
seedPool.description: Pike or perch from a cold lake.
seedPool.narrative: Caught through ice in winter, from a wooden boat in summer.

seedPool.name: Reindeer
seedPool.description: Antlered companion of the north.
seedPool.narrative: Half-wild, half-herded. The Sámi follow them, and the seasons follow both.

seedPool.name: Moss
seedPool.description: Soft green carpet of the boreal floor.
seedPool.narrative: Older than the trees that grow over it.

seedPool.name: Stone
seedPool.description: Granite of the Finnish shield.
seedPool.narrative: Two billion years old, scraped flat by the last ice age.

seedPool.name: Bog
seedPool.description: Spongy peatland that swallows footprints.
seedPool.narrative: A third of the country was once bog. Cloudberries grow only here.

seedPool.name: Steam
seedPool.description: Hot vapor from water on stones.
seedPool.narrative: The original Finnish weather report.

seedPool.name: Wool
seedPool.description: Sheep's coat, spun and ready.
seedPool.narrative: What stands between the body and minus-twenty.

seedPool.name: Wood
seedPool.description: Cut log, dried for the winter stove.
seedPool.narrative: The heat of the country, stacked under every eave.

seedPool.name: Sun
seedPool.description: Summer light that does not set.
seedPool.narrative: For weeks in June, no shadow has time to settle.

conditions.description: Make a ⭐⭐⭐ Discovery
Experience a sauna ritual or its winter-plunge counterpart
Forage a wild food from forest, bog, or shore
Witness the polar light, the long dusk, or the white night
Encounter a piece of Finnish design, craft, or storytelling
Learn a Finnish word for something English doesn't have

seedPool.name: Rice
seedPool.description: The grain that shapes every island meal.
seedPool.narrative: Wet-paddy cultivation arrived two thousand years ago and never left.

seedPool.name: Fish
seedPool.description: Caught from the morning sea.
seedPool.narrative: Before rice, the sea was the larder. After rice, it still was.

seedPool.name: Bamboo
seedPool.description: Hollow green stalk that bends without breaking.
seedPool.narrative: Floor, fence, flute, scaffold, brush. The country's most adaptable material.

seedPool.name: Tea
seedPool.description: Leaf, dried and ready for water.
seedPool.narrative: Brought from the mainland, refined into a ceremony with corners.

seedPool.name: Salt
seedPool.description: Drawn from seawater and sun.
seedPool.narrative: Used to purify, season, and sometimes scatter at a doorway.

seedPool.name: Stone
seedPool.description: Volcanic rock from the islands' bones.
seedPool.narrative: The country was built by tectonics. Its gardens still admit it.

seedPool.name: Wood
seedPool.description: Cedar, pine, or hinoki — fragrant and slow to rot.
seedPool.narrative: Temples and houses share the same skeleton.

seedPool.name: Silk
seedPool.description: Thread spun by the cocooned moth.
seedPool.narrative: A whole town in the mountains used to listen for the worms eating mulberry leaves.

seedPool.name: Mountain
seedPool.description: Steep, layered, often smoking.
seedPool.narrative: Three quarters of the country slopes. The flat parts are where the rice goes.

seedPool.name: Sea
seedPool.description: Salt water surrounding the islands on all sides.
seedPool.narrative: The country is never more than a few hours from a coast.

seedPool.name: Iron
seedPool.description: Ore from the mountain, ready for the forge.
seedPool.narrative: The blade-smiths of Bizen and Soshu folded it into legend.

seedPool.name: Clay
seedPool.description: Earth waiting to be wheeled and fired.
seedPool.narrative: The country invented its own pottery before it had a name.

seedPool.name: Paper
seedPool.description: Mulberry-fiber sheets, hand-pressed and translucent.
seedPool.narrative: Strong enough to be a window, soft enough to be a sliding wall.

seedPool.name: Mist
seedPool.description: Morning fog rolling between mountain shoulders.
seedPool.narrative: Painters left blank paper for it. Photographers wait for it.

seedPool.name: Blossom
seedPool.description: Five pink petals on a bare branch.
seedPool.narrative: A reminder that the most beautiful thing only lasts five days.

conditions.description: Make a ⭐⭐⭐ Discovery
Visit a temple, shrine, or sacred threshold
Sit through a meal that is itself a craft
Witness a contrast between centuries-old and right-now
Travel by rail at a speed that flatters the country
Notice a small kindness encoded into ordinary infrastructure

seedPool.name: Wheat
seedPool.description: Golden grain of the Beauce plain.
seedPool.narrative: France was the wheat basket of Rome. The fields haven't moved much since.

seedPool.name: Grape
seedPool.description: Cluster from a hillside vine.
seedPool.narrative: Burgundy, Bordeaux, Loire, Rhône — the country mapped by what its slopes will grow.

seedPool.name: Milk
seedPool.description: From cow, goat, or sheep.
seedPool.narrative: Two hundred and forty-six varieties of cheese begin here.

seedPool.name: Salt
seedPool.description: Hand-raked from Atlantic flats.
seedPool.narrative: Guérande and Île de Ré: the two coasts that season everyone else's table.

seedPool.name: Honey
seedPool.description: From bees that worked the lavender or the chestnut.
seedPool.narrative: The flavor changes with the field. The bees know the route.

seedPool.name: Egg
seedPool.description: From the henhouse behind the farm.
seedPool.narrative: The mother sauce begins here. So does the soufflé, the omelette, the crème.

seedPool.name: Stone
seedPool.description: Limestone from the Loire or basalt from the Massif.
seedPool.narrative: The country is built from its own quarries. The cathedrals were the first long-term project.

seedPool.name: Iron
seedPool.description: Ore for forges, cathedrals, and one famous tower.
seedPool.narrative: Lorraine produced the steel; Eiffel turned it lacework.

seedPool.name: Wood
seedPool.description: Oak from a forest planted for ships and aged into barrels.
seedPool.narrative: Colbert ordered oaks planted in 1670 so the navy would have masts in 1870. The forests still stand.

seedPool.name: River
seedPool.description: Seine, Loire, Rhône, Garonne.
seedPool.narrative: Each one carved a region with its own dialect, food, and patron saint.

seedPool.name: Lavender
seedPool.description: Purple flower row in summer Provence.
seedPool.narrative: Planted along the Plateau de Valensole. The bees show up by July.

seedPool.name: Olive
seedPool.description: Pressed for oil or cured in brine.
seedPool.narrative: South of a line drawn through Lyon, the Mediterranean takes over the kitchen.

seedPool.name: Garlic
seedPool.description: Bulb that flavors everything south of the Loire.
seedPool.narrative: Pink in Lautrec, white in Provence. Always on the cutting board.

seedPool.name: Butter
seedPool.description: Salted from Brittany, sweet from Normandy.
seedPool.narrative: The map of France's cuisine is the map of where butter is used and where olive oil takes over.

seedPool.name: Cliff
seedPool.description: White chalk dropping into the Channel.
seedPool.narrative: Étretat, Calais, the Normandy coast. Painters camped here for the light.

conditions.description: Make a ⭐⭐⭐ Discovery
Sit at a café long enough to be ignored by the waiter
Taste a regional pairing of bread, cheese, or wine
Stand before a work of art that has outlasted its maker by centuries
Walk a street that was paved before electricity
Cross from city into countryside and feel the language soften

seedPool.name: Corn
seedPool.description: Tall summer crop of the Midwest.
seedPool.narrative: Three sisters, then the staple, then the feed, then the fuel.

seedPool.name: Wheat
seedPool.description: Golden grain of the Plains.
seedPool.narrative: Kansas to the Dakotas — the breadbasket runs north all the way to Canada.

seedPool.name: Cattle
seedPool.description: Range-fed herd of the open West.
seedPool.narrative: Driven up the Chisholm Trail to the railhead. The cowboy was a temp job that became a costume.

seedPool.name: Cotton
seedPool.description: White boll plucked by the bushel.
seedPool.narrative: The first global American crop. The history is heavy and the fiber is light.

seedPool.name: Iron
seedPool.description: Ore from the Mesabi Range.
seedPool.narrative: It became the rails, the bridges, the skyscrapers, the cars.

seedPool.name: Oil
seedPool.description: Crude from a Texas pumpjack.
seedPool.narrative: Spindletop, 1901, ten days of black geyser. The century took the hint.

seedPool.name: Coal
seedPool.description: Black rock dug from Appalachian veins.
seedPool.narrative: Steel was the verb. Coal was the verb's verb.

seedPool.name: Wood
seedPool.description: Lumber from Pacific Northwest stands.
seedPool.narrative: Douglas fir, Sitka spruce. The frontier shipped its trees east before it shipped its cattle.

seedPool.name: Stone
seedPool.description: Granite from New Hampshire, marble from Vermont.
seedPool.narrative: The country's monuments are mostly cut from its own quarries.

seedPool.name: River
seedPool.description: The Mississippi, draining most of a continent.
seedPool.narrative: Mark Twain learned to read it like a book. The country has been quoting him ever since.

seedPool.name: Mountain
seedPool.description: Rocky, Appalachian, Sierra, Cascade.
seedPool.narrative: Two ranges hold the coasts; one runs down the spine.

seedPool.name: Coast
seedPool.description: Atlantic on one side, Pacific on the other, Gulf below.
seedPool.narrative: Three coasts, three cuisines, three ways to take a vacation.

seedPool.name: Tobacco
seedPool.description: Broad leaf cured in a Virginia barn.
seedPool.narrative: The first cash crop of the colonies. The first thing the world bought from America.

seedPool.name: Bison
seedPool.description: Massive beast of the Plains, once thirty million strong.
seedPool.narrative: The herd shaped the prairie. The prairie shaped the country.

seedPool.name: Salt
seedPool.description: Mined from Kansas, panned from Utah.
seedPool.narrative: Salt Lake names itself. The Erie Canal floated on a salt-tax surplus.

conditions.description: Make a ⭐⭐⭐ Discovery
Drive a route long enough that the radio stations change twice
Eat a regional dish that locals are loud about
Stand inside a landscape too big for any photograph
Hear a piece of music born in this country
Witness the contrast between a small town and a big city in the same week

seedPool.name: Mana
seedPool.description: Raw arcane energy, drawn from the ley lines.
seedPool.narrative: The sorcerer's first lesson: it is not free, and it is not yours.

seedPool.name: Light
seedPool.description: The holy radiance the Naaru sing into being.
seedPool.narrative: Not a metaphor here. It heals, it burns, it judges.

seedPool.name: Fel
seedPool.description: Green corruption from beyond the veil.
seedPool.narrative: It works. That is the whole problem with it.

seedPool.name: Frost
seedPool.description: Icy magic from the spires of Northrend.
seedPool.narrative: The cold that Lich Kings teach themselves to enjoy.

seedPool.name: Fire
seedPool.description: Elemental flame, summoned or struck.
seedPool.narrative: Ragnaros sleeps in the deep places. Most fires you meet are the lesser kind.

seedPool.name: Iron
seedPool.description: Ore for the dwarven forges of Ironforge.
seedPool.narrative: Folded by hammer-strikes into the blades the world remembers.

seedPool.name: Copper
seedPool.description: Soft metal of the apprentice smith.
seedPool.narrative: The first ore every blacksmith trains on. The first weight in any backpack.

seedPool.name: Stone
seedPool.description: Cut block of Khaz Modan granite or Stormwind marble.
seedPool.narrative: Cathedrals, fortresses, dwarven halls. The world is built on it.

seedPool.name: Wood
seedPool.description: Felled timber from Elwynn or Teldrassil.
seedPool.narrative: Some trees on this world remember when they were druids. Most do not.

seedPool.name: Cloth
seedPool.description: Linen, wool, or the first bolt a tailor weaves.
seedPool.narrative: A robe begins here. So does a banner.

seedPool.name: Leather
seedPool.description: Hide tanned for armor or saddle.
seedPool.narrative: Skinned and worked by hand. The hunter's first profession.

seedPool.name: Beast
seedPool.description: Wild creature of plains, forest, or mountain.
seedPool.narrative: Wolf, bear, raptor, kodo. Hunted, tamed, occasionally befriended.

seedPool.name: Herb
seedPool.description: Silverleaf, peacebloom, mageroyal — basic alchemy stock.
seedPool.narrative: Every potion in the world began as a leaf someone bent down to pick.

seedPool.name: Crystal
seedPool.description: Faceted shard that holds the arcane.
seedPool.narrative: Mage towers grow them. Some sing if you listen.

seedPool.name: Bone
seedPool.description: Trophy of the hunt, or component of the dark.
seedPool.narrative: Necromancers have one use for it. Druids have another. Soup is not out of the question.

conditions.description: Make a ⭐⭐⭐ Discovery
Pledge to a faction, an order, or a long quarrel
Master an arcane, holy, or fel discipline
Tame, ride, or befriend a creature larger than you are
Stand against an old evil that the kingdoms still whisper about
Find a city built by hands that aren't human
